name: result_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_1_result_equals_base(arch: ArchExec, layer: usize, base: usize)
    requires
        arch@.inv(),
        layer < arch@.layers.len(),
        base <= MAX_BASE,
        0usize <= X86_NUM_ENTRIES,
    ensures
        arch@.next_entry_base(layer as nat, base as nat, 0nat) == base as nat,
{
}
reason: If next_entry_base with idx=0 equals base, entries would start at the same address as the base, meaning no address space is actually mapped by the first entry.

name: result_less_than_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_result_less_than_base(arch: ArchExec, layer: usize, base: usize, idx: usize)
    requires
        arch@.inv(),
        layer < arch@.layers.len(),
        base <= MAX_BASE,
        idx <= X86_NUM_ENTRIES,
    ensures
        arch@.next_entry_base(layer as nat, base as nat, idx as nat) < base as nat,
{
}
reason: If next_entry_base returned a value less than base, page table entries would map backwards into already-mapped address space, corrupting translations.

name: entry_size_zero
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_size_zero(arch: Arch, layer: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(layer) == 0nat,
{
}
reason: If inv permitted zero-sized entries, page table walks would never make progress and every entry would degenerate to a zero-length region.

name: layer_sizes_inverted
code:
proof fn phi_4_layer_sizes_inverted(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() >= 2,
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(0) <= arch.entry_size(1),
{
}
reason: If coarser layers had entry sizes no larger than finer layers, the hierarchical decomposition would be flat or inverted, breaking multi-level address translation.

name: num_entries_always_one
code:
proof fn phi_5_num_entries_always_one(arch: Arch, layer: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.num_entries(layer) <= 1nat,
{
}
reason: If every layer were limited to a single entry, the page table could only map one contiguous region per level, making the entire translation structure useless.
